data.executiveSummary: The qa-login service experienced a significant increase in error rates due to Redis connection timeouts, leading to failures in session store retrieval.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeout
rootCause.description: The root cause of the incident was identified as a Redis connection timeout, which resulted in the qa-login service failing to retrieve session data from the store. This caused numerous LoginExceptions.

evidenceChain: Prometheus metric query indicated a significant number of errors with 2443 HTTP 500 status codes in the last 5 hours.
Loki logs showed multiple instances of 'LoginException in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.'

timeline.event: First recorded Redis connection timeout in the Loki logs
Last recorded Redis connection timeout in the Loki logs

data.impact: Users attempting to log in experienced failures as their session data could not be retrieved, leading to an error rate of 29.88% over the last 5 hours.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Consider restarting the Redis instance to clear any potential orphaned connections, as resolved in similar incident KAN-6.
Implement monitoring and alerting for Redis connection latency and failures to proactively manage such issues.
